results.title: Prometheus | Firecrawl
results.content:
# Prometheus — turn plain-English data requests into Firecrawl collectors

a Titan in Greek mythology who aided humanity by stealing fire from the Olympian gods and giving it to mortals — the gift of fire symbolizing knowledge, technology, and the foundations of civilization.

## How Prometheus works

1. 1 / Ask

   ### Define the request.

   Describe the data you want in plain English. Prometheus uses Firecrawl to collect it.
2. 2 / Keep

   ### Keep the code.

   A genuine Firecrawl SDK collector plus the sample data it produced. Reproducible, versionable, and entirely yours.
3. 3 / Deploy

   ### Or leave it with us.

   Prometheus runs it on a schedule, heals it when it breaks, and drops the data wherever you want it.
4. Your account

   ### Connect your Firecrawl account.

Try

results.content:
# Firecrawl Introduces Prometheus: A Forward-Deployed Agent for Web Data : r/AIinBusinessNews
Skip to main contentFirecrawl Introduces Prometheus: A Forward-Deployed Agent for Web Data : r/AIinBusinessNews

Open menu Open navigation, aims to fix that. Instead of writing custom code or fighting with shifting CSS selectors, you just tell it what data you want in plain English (e.g., _"give me the top 5 stories on Hacker News"_).

How it works:

   Build: It drives a headless browser, figures out the site layout, writes a TypeScript script, tests it in a sandbox, and hands you the working code. [...] Script & Self-Heal: If you host it with Firecrawl and the target website changes its layout, Prometheus automatically re-analyzes the new DOM, rewrites the code, and updates the version history—meaning zero manual maintenance for broken scrapers.

   Deploy: You can trigger it via an API or set it up on a continuous Cron schedule.

results.title: Docs — Prometheus
results.content:
PrometheusDocs

Docsfirecrawl.dev

Get started

# Introduction

Turn a plain-English data request into verified, reproducible Firecrawl code — and keep it fresh.

Prometheus takes a request like "the top 5 Hacker News stories with title, url, and points" and returns a TypeScript collector (`script.ts`) that uses the Firecrawl SDK and prints JSON, plus a sample of the data it produced. It runs the collector before returning, so the code is known to work. Another agent — or you — embeds that code directly.

Optionally, save the collector as a Script — versioned, self-healing when the target site changes — and give it Deployments: a deployment re-collects on a cron schedule, serves fresh data on demand as an API endpoint, or both.

## Three operations [...] ## How it works

`POST /api/v1/build` drives the Prometheus agent headlessly — it experiments against the live site with Firecrawl (search, scrape, map, crawl, interact), authors a deterministic `script.ts`, runs it to verify the output, and only then returns. There is no human in the loop, so instead of asking clarifying questions the agent makes reasonable assumptions and reports them in `howItWorks`.

Scripts wrap that collector in versioning plus Firecrawl's monitoring and self-heal machinery; deployments add the schedule or on-demand trigger. When a run fails because a site changed, Prometheus re-invokes the agent to repair (or rebuild) the collector and appends the corrected version — every track-latest deployment picks it up, no intervention needed. [...] ## Quick start

Ask for a collector over HTTP:

bash

```
curl -s  \curl -s  \ -H 'content-type: application/json' \ -H 'content-type: application/json' \ -d '{"prompt":"top 5 Hacker News stories with title, url, points"}' \ -d '{"prompt":"top 5 Hacker News stories with title, url, points"}' \| jq '{rowCount, script}'| jq '{rowCount, script}'
```

You get back `{ sessionId, script, sample, rowCount, howItWorks, integration, … }`. Write `script` to a file, `npm i @mendable/firecrawl-js`, set `FIRECRAWL_API_KEY`, and run it with `tsx script.ts` — it prints the same shape as `sample`.

Prefer the command line? The CLI wraps the same call:

bash

```
prometheus build "top 5 Hacker News stories with title, url, points"prometheus build "top 5 Hacker News stories with title, url, points"
```

# Is Prometheus by Firecrawl Legit? — Trust Data Profile

## Quick verdict: Prometheus by Firecrawl earns a trust grade A, 85 out of 100, based on 3 of 5 dimensions.

A

85/100

What we found

A Forward Deployed Agent for web data.

AI Agents & Automation · firecrawl.dev · Data updated

Automated data profile from public signals (company registry, policies, review platforms). A full review hasn't been published yet.

| Company & Identity | 6 / 8 |  | Automated signals |
| Data & Privacy | 7.5 / 10 |  | Automated signals |
| Billing & Subscription | 12 / 12 |  | Automated signals |
| Product Reality | Not yet tested |
| Community Reputation | Not yet tested |

## How Prometheus by Firecrawl compares

Among the 4 AI Agents & Automation tools we track, Prometheus by Firecrawl scores higher on trust than 50% of them. Its domain is 2 years old — older than 75% of them, a sign of an established operator.

Computed from our own database of assessed AI Agents & Automation tools — updated as we track more.

## Policy Changes

No policy changes detected since we started tracking this tool.

## Does Prometheus by Firecrawl train AI on your data? [...] ## Verified User Reports

No verified user reports on file for this tool.

## Prometheus by Firecrawl FAQ

Is Prometheus by Firecrawl legit?
:   Prometheus by Firecrawl earns a trust grade of A (85/100) on our evidence-based assessment, based on 3 of 5 trust dimensions we could verify from public records. This is an automated profile — we have not done a hands-on review.

Does Prometheus by Firecrawl share or sell your data?
:   Their privacy policy says: "We may collect, store, and share this personal information with third parties, but only in the ways we explain in this policy."

import Firecrawl from "@mendable/firecrawl-js"; import  as cheerio from "cheerio"; import { parseArgs } from "node:util"; const apiKey = process.env.FIRECRAWL_API_KEY; if (!apiKey) { console.error("FIRECRAWL_API_KEY is not set"); process.exit(1); } const firecrawl = new Firecrawl({ apiKey }); const { values: flags } = parseArgs({ strict: true, options: { "max-pages": { type: "string" }, // cap on bookmark pages to scrape (default 40) }, }); const maxPages = Number(flags["max-pages"] ?? "40"); if (!Number.isFinite(maxPages) || maxPages < 1) { console.error("--max-pages must be a positive number"); process.exit(1); } const PAGE_RE = /^https:\/\/start\.me\/p\/([A-Za-z0-9]+)\/[^"#?]+/; function pageId(url: string): string | null { const m = url.match(PAGE_RE); return m ? m : null; } async [...] const m = url.match(PAGE_RE); return m ? m : null; } async function getRawHtml(url: string): Promise { const doc: any = await firecrawl.scrape(url, { formats: ["rawHtml"], integration: "prometheus", }); const html = doc?.rawHtml ?? doc?.data?.rawHtml ?? ""; if (!html) throw new Error(`no rawHtml returned for ${url}`); return html; } // Collect start.me/p/ page links from an HTML document. function extractPageLinks($: cheerio.CheerioAPI): string[] { const out = new Set(); $("a[href]").each((_, el) => { const href = $(el).attr("href") || ""; if (PAGE_RE.test(href)) out.add(href.split("#").split("?")); }); return [...out]; } // Extract the bookmarks (external links) listed on a bookmark page. function extractBookmarks($: cheerio.CheerioAPI): { url: string; title: string }[] { const out: { [...] PrometheusMarketplace

## Similar Products [...] Eric Ciarla
Firecrawl

Firecrawl

@ansari\_adin good points. this is just an experiment but an SLA may come in the future!

Dhiraj Patel

This is neat. When page structures change, does Prometheus auto-detect the break or wait for you to flag it?

Eric Ciarla
Firecrawl

Firecrawl

@dhiraj\_patel5 it automatically flags and fixes!

Achuth Reddy Bangaru

Prometheus generating the collector code is cool, but the part I’d love to see is whether it also generates validation checks with it. For example, after it samples the data, can it create simple assertions like required fields, expected value ranges, stable selectors, or row counts so teams can catch bad runs before the data reaches downstream systems?

Binu George [...] Eric Ciarla
Firecrawl

Firecrawl

@anand\_thakkar1 Yes that is my favorite part! We decide based on the historical differences in data

Cody Menefee

@anand\_thakkar1 Would love to test one of your more brittle scraping processes on Prometheus!

Ansari Adin

the run it yourself or let us host split is interesting because those are pretty different value propositions. running it yourself means you're still responsible for maintenance even if the agent writes the code. hosting means you're trusting Firecrawl to maintain the extraction logic as sites change, which is a significant operational commitment to make on someone else's behalf. curious what the SLA looks like for hosted maintenance and what happens when a site change breaks extraction and you need the data today
